failure_modes.text: Premature action blurs diagnosis and solution so the system reacts before it understands what is failing.
failure_modes.mitigation: Strictly separate the five adaptive steps: set goals, identify problems, diagnose root causes, design changes, then execute — never blur stages.

failure_modes.text: Anchoring on one perspective makes the team miss the broader system picture it needs to adapt to.
failure_modes.source_quote: Failure to See the Whole Picture
failure_modes.mitigation: Cultivate intellectual humility and stress-test thinking through multiple frames; seek perspectives that challenge the current viewpoint.

failure_modes.text: Stress and emotional hijacking reduce the mental energy available for thoughtful course-correction.
failure_modes.mitigation: Use journaling or reflective practice to separate the emotional reaction from the analytical response before choosing the next adaptive move.

premortem_questions.text: What is the one-day answer right now, and which signal would force us to revise it?
What would we have to believe for this adaptation path to still make sense six months from now?
premortem_questions.source_quote: What would you have to believe?

premortem_questions.text: Are we adapting to real environmental feedback or just defending the last move in a new language?
premortem_questions.source_quote: feedback is interpreted to confirm the current adaptation path

heuristics.text: Decouple goals, diagnosis, design, and execution so each stage can do its own job.
Cultivate intellectual humility and stress-test the current path with believable critics.
heuristics.source_quote: Assume you are wrong until your thinking has been stress-tested

heuristics.text: Satisfice once the next workable move is clear instead of waiting for a perfect adaptive plan.

curation_notes.summary: Chunk set stays close to the source's strongest failure modes, premortem questions, and repeatable heuristics.
donor_drops: Did not widen the chunk surface beyond clearly source-backed failure, premortem, and heuristic material.
open_questions: Some chunk text is compressed for operational reuse, but each item stays traceable to explicit source language.

allies: Relation-rich ally material was left for Wave 3 instead of being smuggled into chunk text.
antagonists: Bias and conflict material stronger as first-order relations was deferred to Wave 3.
structured_tensions: Any higher-order candidacy remains observational only in batch artifacts.
